legalnotice.para: This work is licensed under MIT OR Apache-2.0 license.

revision.revremark: Initial release of opcgw user manual

abstract.para: opcgw is a Rust-based gateway that bridges ChirpStack LoRaWAN Network Server with OPC UA clients for industrial automation and SCADA systems. This manual provides comprehensive guidance on installation, configuration, operation, and troubleshooting of the opcgw gateway.

section.title: What is opcgw?
section.para: opcgw (OPC Gateway) is a middleware application that bridges the gap between LoRaWAN sensor networks and industrial automation systems. It provides seamless integration between:

listitem.para: ChirpStack LoRaWAN Network Server — Manages LoRaWAN devices and gathers metrics from distributed IoT sensors
OPC UA Servers — Presents data to SCADA systems, industrial controllers, and human-machine interfaces

section.para: The gateway continuously polls device metrics from ChirpStack, stores them in a local SQLite database, and exposes them via OPC UA for real-time consumption by industrial clients.

listitem.para: Real-time Data Collection — Continuous polling of metrics from ChirpStack at configurable intervals
Persistent Storage — SQLite-backed storage with UPSERT semantics for current metrics and append-only audit logs for historical data
OPC UA Server — Native OPC UA 1.04 endpoint for seamless SCADA integration
Graceful Degradation — Continues operation even during database corruption, access failures, or partial data loss
Comprehensive Logging — Structured logging via tracing for operational visibility and debugging
Data Retention Policies — Automatic pruning of historical data based on retention configuration

section.title: System Architecture
section.para: opcgw follows a modular architecture with clear separation of concerns:

listitem.para: Poller Module (chirpstack.rs) — Authenticates to ChirpStack gRPC API, polls metrics at regular intervals, transforms data into internal format
Storage Layer (storage module) — Abstracts database backend with pluggable implementations (in-memory for testing, SQLite for production)
OPC UA Server (opc_ua.rs) — Exposes metrics as OPC UA variables, handles subscription requests
Configuration System (config.rs) — TOML-based configuration with environment variable overrides
Any 64-bit processor (x86-64, ARM64) with 2+ cores recommended for production
Minimum 512 MB RAM; 2 GB recommended for deployments with 100+ devices
Minimum 100 MB free disk space; 1 GB recommended if retaining historical data
Network connectivity to ChirpStack server (gRPC port 8080) and OPC UA clients
OS: Linux (Debian, Ubuntu, CentOS), macOS, or Windows with Docker
Rust: v1.87.0 or later (if building from source)
ChirpStack: v4.0 or later with gRPC API enabled
Network: Firewall rules allowing inbound connections to OPC UA port (default 4855)

section.title: Installation via Docker (Recommended)
section.para: Docker installation is the quickest way to get opcgw running without compiling from source.

step.para: Ensure Docker is installed on your system. Visit Docker documentation for installation instructions.

note.title: Docker Volumes
note.para: The Docker image uses two volumes:

listitem.para: /app/config — Configuration files (TOML, logging setup)
/app/data — SQLite database files and PKI certificates

section.title: Building from Source
section.para: If you prefer to build opcgw from source or need platform-specific optimizations:

step.para: The compiled binary will be at target/release/opcgw

note.title: Build Time
note.para: Release builds may take 5-10 minutes depending on your system and internet connection. Debug builds are faster but unoptimized.

section.title: Configuration Overview
section.para: opcgw uses TOML configuration files for all settings. The main configuration file is typically located at config/config.toml. Environment variables can override TOML settings.

section.title: Global Configuration
section.para: The [global] section contains general gateway settings:
section.programlisting:
[global] # Log level: trace, debug, info, warn, error log_level = "info"
# OPC UA endpoint URL (default: opc.tcp://localhost:4855) opc_ua_endpoint = "opc.tcp://0.0.0.0:4855"
# Data retention in days (0 = no pruning) retention_days = 30

section.title: ChirpStack Configuration
section.para: The [chirpstack] section configures connection to your ChirpStack server:
section.programlisting:
[chirpstack] # ChirpStack server address (gRPC endpoint) server_address = "chirpstack.example.com:8080"
# API authentication token (obtain from ChirpStack) api_token = "your_api_token_here"
# Tenant ID (UUID) tenant_id = "ffffffff-ffff-ffff-ffff-ffffffffffff"
# Poll interval in seconds poll_interval_secs = 30
# Maximum retries on connection failure max_retries = 3
# Request timeout in seconds request_timeout_secs = 10

warning.title: API Token Security
warning.para: Never commit the API token to version control. Use environment variable overrides for sensitive credentials:

section.title: OPC UA Configuration
section.para: The [opcua] section configures the OPC UA server:
section.programlisting:
[opcua] # OPC UA server endpoint endpoint = "opc.tcp://0.0.0.0:4855"
# Server name server_name = "opcgw"
# Product URI product_uri = "https://github.com/guycorbaz/opcgw"
# Enable PKI/security (true = requires certificates) pki_enabled = false
# PKI directory (contains certs and keys) pki_dir = "./pki"

section.title: Application and Device Configuration
section.para: Applications and their devices are configured via application sections:
section.programlisting:
[[application]] # Application name (from ChirpStack) name = "smart_agriculture"
# Namespace prefix in OPC UA (address space) namespace = "SmartAg"
# Devices in this application [[application.device]] name = "field_sensor_01" device_eui = "0102030405060708"
# Metrics from this device [[application.device.metric]] metric_name = "temperature" metric_type = "Float" opc_ua_node_id = "ns=2;s=field_sensor_01/temperature"
[[application.device.metric]] metric_name = "humidity" metric_type = "Int" opc_ua_node_id = "ns=2;s=field_sensor_01/humidity"

section.title: Logging Configuration
section.para: Logging is configured via config/log4rs.yaml:
section.programlisting:
refresh_rate: 30 seconds
appenders: console: kind: console encoder: pattern: "[{d(%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S)}] {l} - {m}\n"
file: kind: file path: "logs/opcgw.log" encoder: pattern: "[{d(%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S)}] {l} [{t}] {m}\n"
root: level: info appenders: - console - file
loggers: opcgw: level: debug chirpstack: level: info

section.title: Startup Verification
section.para: After starting opcgw, verify that it is running correctly by checking the logs. Look for messages indicating:

listitem.para: Connection to ChirpStack server established
OPC UA server started on the configured endpoint
Initial metric polling completed

example.title: Expected Startup Log Output
example.programlisting: 2026-04-20T12:34:56.789Z INFO opcgw: Starting opcgw v1.0.0 2026-04-20T12:34:56.890Z INFO chirpstack: Connecting to chirpstack.example.com:8080 2026-04-20T12:34:57.123Z INFO chirpstack: Connected to ChirpStack server 2026-04-20T12:34:57.234Z INFO opcua: Starting OPC UA server on opc.tcp://0.0.0.0:4855 2026-04-20T12:34:57.456Z INFO opcua: OPC UA server started successfully 2026-04-20T12:34:58.567Z INFO chirpstack: Poll cycle 1: 15 devices, 60 metrics collected

section.title: Graceful Shutdown
section.para: The gateway supports graceful shutdown via SIGTERM signal. On shutdown, it will:

listitem.para: Stop accepting new OPC UA client connections
Allow existing clients to disconnect gracefully
Flush all pending database writes
Close the database connection

section.para: To gracefully stop the gateway:

warning.title: Force Shutdown
warning.para: Avoid using SIGKILL (-9) as it may leave the database in an inconsistent state. Always prefer SIGTERM for graceful shutdown.

section.title: Monitoring Metrics
section.para: Monitor these key indicators during normal operation:

listitem.para: Time to complete a full metric poll cycle. Should be < poll_interval_secs.
Number of devices with recent metric updates. Should match expected device count.
Monitor SQLite database file size. Should grow slowly if retention is configured.
Number of active OPC UA client connections. Monitor for unexpected spikes.

section.title: Health Checks
section.para: Implement health checks to verify gateway operation:

step.para: Connect to the OPC UA server using an OPC UA client tool
Browse the address space and verify devices are present
Check metric values are recent (updated within last poll interval)
Review logs for any error messages or warnings

section.title: Cannot Connect to ChirpStack
section.para: Symptoms: Logs show connection failures to ChirpStack server

listitem.para: Verify ChirpStack server address and port are correct in configuration
Confirm ChirpStack is running: curl http://chirpstack.example.com:8080
Check network connectivity: ping chirpstack.example.com
Verify firewall rules allow outbound connections to port 8080
Test gRPC connectivity: grpcurl -plaintext list chirpstack.example.com:8080
Verify API token is valid and has appropriate permissions

section.title: Database Corruption or Access Errors
section.para: Symptoms: Errors accessing the SQLite database, "database disk image malformed"

listitem.para: Stop the gateway gracefully
Check disk space: df -h
Backup the database: cp data/opcgw.db data/opcgw.db.backup
Verify database integrity: sqlite3 data/opcgw.db "PRAGMA integrity_check;"
If corrupted, the gateway will automatically recreate on startup with graceful degradation

section.title: OPC UA Clients Cannot Connect
section.para: Symptoms: OPC UA client connection attempts are refused

listitem.para: Verify OPC UA server is running: Check logs for "OPC UA server started"
Check firewall rules allow inbound on OPC UA port (default 4855)
Verify client is connecting to correct endpoint: opc.tcp://gateway-host:4855
Test local connectivity: netstat -tlnp | grep 4855
If PKI is enabled, verify client certificates are in trusted store

section.title: Metrics Not Updating from ChirpStack
section.para: Symptoms: Metrics appear in OPC UA but values are stale

listitem.para: Verify devices are configured correctly in config.toml
Confirm devices are active in ChirpStack (not disabled)
Check poll interval is reasonable (default 30 seconds)
Review logs for poll cycle messages
Verify ChirpStack is collecting data from LoRaWAN devices

section.title: Database Maintenance
section.para: Regular database maintenance ensures optimal performance:

section.title: Historical Data Pruning
section.para: If data retention is enabled, old historical data is automatically pruned based on the retention_days configuration. To manually control pruning:

listitem.para: Set retention_days = 0 to disable automatic pruning
Monitor database size to ensure pruning is working
Historical metrics are preserved in metric_history table

section.title: Log Rotation
section.para: Configure log rotation to prevent log files from consuming excessive disk space. Update config/log4rs.yaml to use rolling file appender:
section.programlisting: appenders: file: kind: rolling_file path: "logs/opcgw.log" policy: trigger: kind: size limit: 104857600 # 100 MB roller: kind: fixed_window pattern: "logs/opcgw.{}.log" count: 10

row.entry: Log level: trace, debug, info, warn, error
Historical data retention in days (0 = no pruning)

listitem.para: Check network connectivity and server address
Database corruption detected; will be recreated on next startup

varlistentry.term: OPC UA server failed to start

listitem.para: Check port is available and not in use; verify firewall rules
Open-source LoRaWAN Network Server that manages devices and collects metrics
Long-Range Wide-Area Network protocol for low-power IoT devices
OPC Unified Architecture, a machine-to-machine communication protocol for industrial automation
Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition system for monitoring and controlling industrial processes
Remote Procedure Call framework built on HTTP/2 for high-performance APIs
Lightweight embedded SQL database engine used for opcgw storage
